Key success factors 51 3.1.2 Primary SWOT Analysis 54 3.2 Form of entry 59 3.2.1 Export as an entry strategy 65 3.2.2 Business model canvas adaptation 66 3.3 Evaluation of strategy 71 Conclusion 77 References 79 Appendix 87 I n troduction Goal and objectives The paper examine s  the managerial problem faced by the company  Soyuzblankizdat , pulp and paper producer in the city of Saint Petersburg,  and potential solution to it.  LLC «SBI» operates in Russia for more than 25 years and does not have any novel growth strategies on the local market, that is why it needs to expand geographically with the aim of increasing its profits and gaining recognition in a foreign market. The motives for entering a new market also include the subjective desire of the company's management to realize their own knowledge  &  skills to expand their business beyond the domestic market and the prestige of doing international business. Thus, t he  goal  of this paper is to  define  the optimal market for the entry by SBI and  develop  the entry strategy. Objectives   include : provision of  a detailed description of the company; managerial   problem  identification ; research methodology description and  justif ication ; provision   of   the extensive  selection of entry markets; identification of  two best options for market entry; analys is of  their micro- and macroenvironments; target  market  identification ;  analysis of  the competitive position and strategy of the company; development of  the entry strategy; evaluati on of  the strategy effectiveness .  Object and subject The research object is the company itself, LLC ‘ Soyuzblankizdat ’ , the information about which is obtained through a series of semi-structured interviews with the leading economist or the economist assistant .  The goal of the interviews was to extract the necessary data for a further analysis that would allow to identify strong and weak sides of the company, its current market position and best entry strategy, designed specifically for this company.  The subject of this paper is market entry strategy  that is identified with the help of secondary sources. These include  scientific articles that provide a deeper understanding and insight into a model or framework, statistical data and reports that would demonstrate the changes over time and trends in the industries, news clippings that are used in order to evaluate  the actual conditions of the political, economic, production and other fields, government data that would be useful in extracting precise information, numbers and so on, and reviews that would be useful in evaluation of environments. Part 1. Characterization of the company, managerial problem and methodology 1.1 Description of the company « SOYUZBLANKIZDAT »  or shortly  « SBI » is a  limited liability  company that focuses on production and wholesale of  statione ry, mainly  paper ,  and cardboard  products.  The company was founded in 1995 and , according to the interview with the representatives of the company, it  quickly  be came  one of the  leader s  in  the  paper prod uction industry first in  the  North -Western region and then  all over  Russia .  At the moment, LLC «SBI» provides its products to main operators of the stationery market to which it has proved to be a reliable and stable partner.  SBI acts as a linkage between raw materials supplier and the customer that sells under its own brand.  Products  and Activities Today, the range of products includes printed products, all kinds of binders, folders with strings and archival  systems, notepads, sets of  colo ured  paper and cardboard, sketchbooks, folders with draugh ting paper, notebooks with  colo ured  and  boom vinyl covers, stationery books in different bindings, including the book one, thus, the range encompasses almost all possible paper products.  According to the charter of SBI, it  executes the following activities: Manufacture of paper and cardboard products;  Non-specialized wholesale trade; Certain types of printing activities;  Production of printing plates and preparatory activities; Bookbinding and finishing activities.  «SBI»  operates  all around Russia, mainly in Central and North-Western regions . It has its warehouses in the area of St. Petersburg from which all the shipments take place.  The company’s  main  customers  are stationery stores and wholesale buyers (retail stores), such as  Komus , B ureaucrat , Space,  OfficeMug ,  and  Haskel . It total,  by  14.05.2021 SBI had 77 wholesale buyers.  Suppliers  and Competitors Main  suppliers   of   «SBI» : the companies that  raw  paper and cardboard products ( paper , offset paper, cardboard, colo red paper and cardboard, corrugated products), e.g.  «MAYAK», « Europaper », « Gophrotorg », «AR-PRINT», «Kama» , and supply them in rolls or as pre-cut produce ;  transportation companies, like  «Express-auto», « EuroPack », « GoldAuto »;  companies that supply special materials, such as  paper clips, braids, etc.,  for a final product, e.g. «Helmet-Plus», « Perchatochka »;  renting services - « Kirovsky  Factory» ;  companies providing thermoresistant equipment, like  « Viking » or « EuroKom »;  typographies  that provide customization to produce , e.g. « Silverprint », « Nevsky  Printing House» and « Interpoligraf ».  Main competitors of  «SBI»   in the market of Russia  are LLC « Evricka » , « Gophrotara » and  « Panse » . They operate in the same industry and offer similar assortment, like colo u red paper, cardboard,  stationery books in different bindings, and so on.  In the interview with  «SBI», its representatives noted  that they compete with each other  on Russian market and take part  in  the same  tenders.  Organizational structure The company has a functional organizational structure with 6 departments, where finance, client relations, sales, HR and product are the main departments  that focus its work on business aspects of the company,  and production (finished products warehouse and factory)  that  is devoted to goods manufacturing.  The company’s personnel accounts for around  70 people, meaning that it is  a small -to-medium  size enterprise.  Organizational structure of 14.12.2020 CEO Finance and Planning Client Relations Sales HR Product Production Leading   economist Key   client   relations   managers Sales   managers HR  manager Designer Finished   products   warehouse Economists Client   relations   managers Labor   protection   specialist Technologist Factory Economist   assistants Senior   cost   economist Source . Interview with the representatives, 2021.  The implemented organizational structure has proved to be efficient for the company, however, it can be seen that the company does not have a separate marketing department. It can be explained by the fact that managers from sales and client relations departments are responsible for already existing clients and for the search of new clients.  It works well for the company, and it does not intend to introduce a marketing department since, according to the leading economist, it is not relevant for this industry.  Financial indicators The main purpose of financial analysis is to obtain the key characteristics that could give an objective and accurate image of financial condition of the enterprise and projection for the future. At the table 2 that shows the parameters of  «SBI» over the last 2 years, 2019 and 2020 , it is indicated that revenues were steadily growing from RUB 263  mn  to RUB 280  mn . However, the net profit decreased by 4% due to the increased cost of sales .   It can be explained by expansion of personnel and costs connected with it. In general, the company shows positive results in its indicators as its net profit ability   is   stable at a rate RUB 3  mn  and higher over past 2 years. Business model description Business Model Canvas Customer segments Customer profiling enables businesses to target and engage with their customers better by determining a clear set of messages and imagery for specific customer segments .  SBI customers are considered to be B2B wholesale buyers and t he company has identified two main customer  segments : Company X.   Chain store,  wholesale buyer,  supplier of goods for office and school, paper, cardboard, et cetera.  Operates all over the country/region.  Orders  customized,  specifically designed products (basic products with authentic prints, fonts, filling).  Company Y .  Wholesale  distributor of goods for home, office and school.  Operates  all over the country and internationally. Orders basic produce for further  self- elaboration  and customization.   There can be minor clients that request one-time  shipments. Those include customers with whom there may be friendly relationships and marketplace platforms that sell  piece by piece (non-wholesale), such as  Wildberries  or Ozon.  Value proposition SBI  provides  its customers  with  a wide range of paper and cardboard products, both customized and basic .  They include paper in A4 format of different density and color; notebooks of different sizes, designs, with different number of pages; flipcharts; archival systems; binders; folders with strings; notepads; sketchbooks and so on.  The company assures the quality of its goods and offers them at a comparatively low price in the Russian market.  Through economies of scale, the company is able to meet the needs of its customers who are price sensitive and require large volumes of products. Channels SBI does not have any specific promotion strategies like advertisement, social media reach, Google/Yandex ads, etc. The company has a sales team that looks for and  directly  contacts potential customers. Moreover, the representatives of the company attend tenders , forums  and fairs where they can offer the products and show the samples of assortment.  Usually, potential customers reach the company themselves after reviewing the information obtained at forums and fairs.   The direct marketing is used for the search of new clients  since the company  acts as a link between the  producers of raw materials  and the end product  seller ( under the brand name of the buyer ) and does not need any other marketing channels. In addition, as SBI is present on the market for more than 20 years and all the potential customers are acquainted with the brand, it has decided to use direct marketing.  Customer relations The contact with the customers is maintained via email, post, telephone, and face-to-face meetings.  These channels are used with the purpose of price reconciliation, ordering, contracting, order tracking and replacements & returns.  Email  and phone are  used for the primary negotiation regarding the prices, availability, contract terms, transportation, ordering, initial confirmation of orders and  exchange & refund procedures.  Telephone is majorly used in order to reach the company, while email is  a better tool since it allows written confirmations.  Post is used with the goal of sending the official, signed  documents or (if it is a new customer) samples distribution.  Face-to-face meetings take place in order for the customer to get to know the company and its representatives. The   customer relations can be divided into the following stages:  Start of communication . Potential client makes a call or sends an email to the company where he or she requests the information about the assortment, availability, price list, characteristics and so on.  Contracting . In order to cooperate, two business  parties  should sign a contract where the terms of  cooperation  are indicated. If any changes incur, the terms are reviewed by both parties.  Reconciliation of prices . Since the prices on raw materials change all the time due to various reasons, such as rise in wood price, change in the production technology, etc., it affects the purchasing power of  customers . That is why the prices are always negotiated to achieve the equilibrium price, suitable for both  SBI  and  a customer . It is done through the e-mail communication where  SBI  has to warn 1 month before the price change.  U sually ,  the price change is equal to 2 to 5 % that seems to be bearable. Ordering . The representatives of  SBI  communicate with  customers  about the amount of needed specific goods and the time needed to  deliver them . At times,  SBI  can be lacking some of the products, making  a customer  change their order composition (that eventually affects the final product output). The order is paid after it has been delivered.  Order tracking .  T he company has to inform the customers about the latest updates on its order. It includes the stock, level of order readiness, loading, transportation, etc.  Defects & replacements/returns . Sometimes, it happens that some parts of the order are defected. In this case,  S BI has to replace the damaged goods on its own cost.  The terms of the policy are communicated and written in the contract individually with each customer.  Revenue streams  The company SBI receives its revenue on  a  contractual basis   depending on a customer. The clients that have been loyal to SBI and make  large  orders on an ongoing basis have an incentive over other clients in terms of installment plan. When working with new customers, SBI has a requirement  for the cust omers to pay upon the delivery.  Key Resources Main resources of the company are its employees  and  manufacturing facilities, both own and rented.  In order to maintain running the business, the company possesses financial resources that are used for paying office rent, production facility rent, salaries, tax, raw materials, transportation, electricity, water, etc.  Humans are the key resource to cooperate with customers and provide all business activities ; they are the reason why the company is successful on the market and has a number of partnerships. As for physical resources, SBI owns equipment used for manufacturing and rents the warehouse and production facility at  Kirovsky   factory .  Key activities Activities of the company can be found in the section ‘Description of the company; Products and Activities’. Other than that,  employees of  SBI perform accounting,  customer service, control and other day-to-day activities .   Key partners Partners of SBI are as follows:  the companies that raw paper and cardboard products  ,  transportation companies,  companies that supply special materials, such as  paper clips, braids, etc.,  for a final product ,  renting services ,  companies providing thermoresistant equipment,   typographies  that provide customization to produce. Moreover, SBI has unique agreements with the producers of raw materials that allow the company be the only (or one of a  few)  distributor (s)   of  a certain type of product.  Cost structure The organization’s costs include  (per month): cost for  labor  that varies according to the competences of employees and their scope of activities   – the average salary (for both office and production site employees) is 30 000 RUB ; production costs, such as rent  of production facility – 1 000 000 RUB ,  communal payments ( electricity  – 50 000 RUB, water (heating) – 200 000 RUB in winter season, 20 000 RUB in summer) ,  raw materials purchase,  use of additional materials; freight, storage and distribution;  registration fees;  IT-related costs (maintenance of software, technical support);  administrative costs that consist of office rent , parking spots,  water and electricity  – all together 100 000 RUB ,  stationery, books  fax, printing, etc. Business Model Canvas 8. Revenue streams Sale of produce on a contractual basis   Source : made by author Managerial   problem   s tatement The paper production industry is well-developed, despite this  the whole paper and card board market continues to inch up, thanks to the packaging and tissue segments . Chipboard production is focused on the needs of the domestic market, while cellulose board is also in demand in foreign markets and a significant part of it is exported. In just 10 months of 2020, the volume of shipments to foreign markets exceeded the five-year maximum by 22%, breaking the mark of 1 million tons . Since «SBI»  includes highly demanded goods, such as cardboard and cellulose products, in its assortment, it has a great potential to become a successful  provider .  Today , about 400 million tons of paper are consumed annually in the world. According to the UN, over the past 40 years this figure has quadrupled. Even the development of electronic document management, the decline in demand for paper press and the decline in paper consumption in schools and offices during the pandemic did not significantly reverse the growth in demand for pulp and paper products. According to the World Paper Markets up to 2030 study prepared by  Poyry  Management Consulting, by 2030 global consumption of paper and paperboard will amount to 482 million tons. Annual demand growth is 1.1% per year .  Thus, expansion of the sector is inevitable and  «SBI»   owns an advantage and will be prospectful in these terms .  According to the scientific journal  CompuArt , around  80% of all  Russian  pulp and paper products are produced by 15  massive  enterprises. Moreover, foreign capital is present at least in every such enterprise. The remaining 160180 enterprises account for 20% of the pro duction, and  «SBI»  is one of these companies.  Russian paper and pulp industry is defined as saturated and i n order to differentiate from medium-size competitors, a  new  strategy  for  «SBI»  of an entry to a foreign market can be developed.  LLC «SBI» operates in Russia for more than 25 years and does not have any  novel  growth strategies on  the local  market,  that is why  it  needs  to expand geographically with the aim of  increasing its profits and gaining recognition in a foreign market.  The motives for entering a  new market also include the subjective desire of the company's management to realize their own knowledge  &  skills to expand their business beyond the domestic market and the prestige of doing international business.  The main challenge  of this paper  is to identify a new optimal market where the realizatio n of activities can take place, choose the type of entry  strategy  and  evaluate the  model for the entry and  potential  operation in a new state.  Methodology   The relevance of a research study and the choice of methods are crucial steps in the research process. Using wrong methodology can negatively reflect on the quality of the paper and show its disadvantages instead of its potential advantages.  Since  this paper is focused on the proposition of entry strategy to a new foreign market, it would be useful to use  both prima ry and secondary sources .   In order to gain an in-depth information and certain insights of the company and industry in general,  a series  of interviews  were conducted  with the representatives of LLC «SBI».  The questions of the interview s  can be found in the appendix.  The information regarding the activities, range of products, financial indicators, and managerial problem proposal was obtained from the leading economist of the company. O ther information about the LLC was acquired with the help of  the  economist assistant  who is familiar with the majority of documents, activities and processes of the company . The interview took place at the main office of the company where the representatives  ga ve answers on posed questions. Moreover, when lacking any data, the online communication through WhatsApp took place.  The goal of the interviews was to extract the necessary data for a further analysis that would allow to identify strong and weak sides of the company, its  current  market position and best entry strategy, designed  specifically  for this company.  The reason why the interviews were conducted with the leading economist and economist assistant is that these employees are well-acquainted with all the processes and mechanisms taking place in the company and could provide in-depth insights on the current state of the company, their goals and reasoning where needed.  The interviews were semi-structured and quite flexible , qualitative in nature , allowing to  switch  to different topics when it was needed. Starting with questions that had been prepared in advance, I could come up with other matters that seem ed   consequential and  not of a less importance.  In addition, the questions could be posed in an unset manner with the possibility of paraphrasing and change of order. Furthermore , the accurate data about the company was provided; it included table of financial indicators,  statute of the company that contained main  activities and legal aspects, and table of partners and customers. Such points, as managerial problem, organizational structure and main products were discussed during the interview.  T his paper contains secondary sources, in particular, scientific articles  that would provide a deeper understanding and insight into a model or framework , statistical data  and reports that would demonstrate the changes over time and trends in the industries , news clippings  that are used in order to evaluate the actual conditions of the political, economic, production and other fields , government data  that  would be useful in extracting precise information, numbers and so on , and reviews  that would be useful in evaluation of environments . These sources could provide both general overview and specific details related to the searched field. Choice of tools In  the market selection  section , t he  choice of markets  is determined by the acquisition of  a sustainable competitive advantage  there . The company’s resources should have value, rarity, indispensability and not be easily imitated .  In order to identify a couple of potential markets, it is first needed to analyse the given by the company cluster of countries in terms of its most crucial factors, like politics, economic benefits, et cetera.  As  long as these  factors for potential market entry  are  discussed and the top two countries  are  chosen, it is necessary to  evaluate   these  two potential markets in depth, taking into account the factors that are related to the local market in general. In this paper, the assessment of both  m a cro- and m i croenvironment of markets will take place.  To evaluate the macroenvironment,  such frameworks as PESTEL analysis, STEP analysis and  EFAS form are considered.  A n  effective result of STEP analysis can be obtained with regular use with time fixing to track the dynamics of changes in indicators as well as their impact, which in turn will accurately assess the external environment of the enterprise . However, it is not preferrable for this paper since the recent political and economic environments are changing daily due to the Russian  military operation  in Ukraine.  To systematize the data obtained as a result of the work on the analysis of strategic environmental factors,  Whellen  T. and Hunger D. suggest the use of the so-called EFAS form (External Strategic Factors Analysis Summary) . It seems to be irrelevant in this paper because there is no def i ned entry strategy by the company on the chosen market to which this framework would be a better fit.   In  this paper  PEST  analysis  will be conducted, which  is a simple and convenient method for analysing the macro environment (external environment) of an enterprise. The PESTEL analysis technique is often used to assess key market trends in an industry .   The letters stand for Political, Economic, Social, Technological, Environmental and Legal. Depending on the organisation, it can be reduced to PEST or some areas can be added (e.g. Ethical) . PEST analysis can be an effective framework to use in the  strategy formation  for  «SBI» .  Environmental and Legal factors are not going to be included inasmuch as, according to  electronic fund of legal and normative-technical documents , the members of EAEU follow interstate environmental and legal standards and norms in paper and cardboard industry .  With the goal to analyze competitive environment in chosen markets, such frameworks as the Value Net or Porter’s 5 forces could be used. The former framework,  also known as Coopetition Framework ,   is an analytical strategy tool that combines strategy and game theory, in order to describe and analyze the behavior of multiple players within a given industry or market .  Nevertheless, it includes only 4 main groups (customers, suppliers, competitors, complementors) that influence the course of any business.  For the microenvironment analysis, such tool as Porter’s Five Forces will be applied for chosen markets.  That is why, this paper contains  Porter's Five Forces  which  is a model that identifies and analyses five competitive forces that shape every industry and helps  to  determine an industry's weaknesses and strengths .  Michael Porter's theory was developed to assess potential risks. It  helps  in  evaluating  possible adverse events that may somehow affect the business in future .  The five forces are bargaining power of suppliers, bargaining power of buyers, threat of substitution, potential new entrants, and rivalry among competitors .  With the purpose  to  enter new market, Key  (Critical)  Success Factors should be analysed in order to understand whether the company will have any incentives to be present on that  market.  KSFs  are the areas of  the  business or project that are vital to its success , they  give people focus, and ensure that tasks and projects are aligned across teams and departments .  When having chosen a final market to enter,  potential business performance of  the company SBI  should be critically assessed through the use of this tool. It will also contribute to identification of strengths and weaknesses of the company on the chosen market.  With the purpose of analysing the chosen market  primary  SWOT  analysis   is going to be used. It will contribute to the evaluation of  opportuni ties and risks, and then strategy proposition  for market entry.  The primary objective of a SWOT analysis is to help organizations develop a full awareness of all the factors involved in making a business decision .  In this paper, the SWOT analysis is a useful tool to facilitate strategic planning process.  SWOT is an acronym for Strengths, Weaknes ses, Opportunities, and Threats, where the former two are the factors of internal environment and the latter two are the ones of external environment.  The SOAR analysis could be an alternative to SWOT.  A SOAR analysis maintains the Strengths and Opportunities sections of a SWOT analysis but introduced Aspirations and Results in the place of Weaknesses and Threats. Aspirations focus on what the organization wants to do, who they want to serve, and where they wish to operate. The results section addresses how the organization will identify and track their progress toward their Aspirations and Opportunities .  Although, this framework mainly focuses on  the proposed strategy and its evaluation, whereas this paper introduces another tool to complete this task – OLI.  OLI  paradigm, developed in 1980 by J. Dunning,  helps companies evaluate the competitive advantages resulting from internationalization and guides expansion to foreign markets .  It includes ownership, location and localization advantages of  the company on a potential market.  This framework is useful to identify the entry strategy itself.  This paper could have had LLL (linkage-leverage-learning) framework developed by  Mathews J.A. ,  which  provides a strategic framework that is focused on accelerated internationalization—creating a light, flexible lattice structure , however, it is mainly used for emerging companies that do not have much experience or expertise.  Finally,  in order to evaluate the proposed strategy Value Creation Chain or Business Model Canvas could be used. This paper applies the latter framework since it gives a better outlook on changes that the company will have to incur  in order to successfully enter a new market .  The business model canvas is a shared language for describing, visualizing, assessing and changing business models. It describes the rationale of how an organization creates, delivers and captures value . The model can demonstrate the level of impact on the company’s affairs  and structure  in case of a new market entry.  PART 2.  S election of countries for potential entry 2.1 Primary selection of countries  for potential entry For the expansion, the company  «SBI» has suggested  to focus  on  a number of  CIS (Commonwealth of Independent States) countries  which   include  Armenia, Azerbaijan, Belarus , Kazakhstan , Moldova, and Ukraine , since there will be little to no language barriers and simplified trade (as the countries are the members of CIS or situated close to Russia which makes it possible to ship products quickly by land).  However, this cluster of countries was suggested before the Russian  military operation  into Ukraine, thus,  the provided analysis about Ukraine will be further irrelevant.   In order to compare  given  foreign  markets,  it is necessary to use proper criteria.  I n this  paper , the y include  Relationship between Russia & a given country, Distance to the country from St. Petersburg warehouse, Transportation costs per 1 truck (to the capital),  Population, and  Legal aspects.  These factors are considered as relevant  (explained further)  and provide primary outlook on the potential business conduction there. They have different weights that are formed due to the level of significance (the higher the %, the greater impact this factor will have on trade and operation of SBI)   that was identified according to subjective opinion of the author .  Relationship between Russia & a given country   –  due to  the  recent political situation in the world, some coun tries have been opposing Russia, its political affairs  and  the  way of  conducting  business, thus, making  the economic relationships  between states  unstable. Such criteria as trade agreements and  level  of the cross-national business  development  should be taken into account.  The bilateral relations can affect the trade across borders in both negative and positive ways. Poor state of  mutual agreement  (such as military conflict)  can imply sanctions that have a constraining impact on trade.  This factor is subjective and is based on my personal opinion from the news clippings  and statistics .  It has a weight of 35% since the political  and economic  instability can cause disruption of cross-national business.  Distance to the  country  from St. Petersburg  warehouse   – as all manufacturing facilities are situated in the region of St. Petersburg, it is necessary to understand  how far to deliver the products and  the risks of lon g distance routes .  The capital of a given country will be considered as a point of destination since more goods are demanded in more populated areas.  Moreover,  often,  the  largest  distribution centres are situated in  capitals or  populous cities , meaning that from there the local networks will operate (« SBI » does not need to deliver its produce to several  distributors across the country).  It has a weight of 10 % because there are certain risks to bear wh en shipping for long distances.  Distance is determined according to the service  Transportica .  Transportation costs per 1 truck (to the capital)  – these costs can differ upon a country due to the different taxation systems, prices for fuel,  visa requirements,  permitted rate of  goods carriage,  and so on .  To include the price of transportation in the cost of selling goods, customers need to calculate the cost of transporting goods across the CIS. This stage is an important part of economic planning, which directly affects the feasibility of commercial activity .  It has a weight of 25 % as company’s strategy of cost leadership can be unachieved when the costs are too high.  Transportation costs  are determined according to the service  Transportica , which conducts the optimal price and takes into account the quality of roads ; the price is calculated for a 20-ton loading  with St. Petersburg as a departure point .  Population   –   the paper products are always in demand as pupils have to write at schools, offices and bureaus need to print, products at stores need to have price tags, and so on. The bigger the population, the higher the chance of having more products ordered.  The data for this factor is taken from the World Bank .  It has a weight of 10%.  Taxation  – there is a chance that the country which the company wants to enter can have restrictions   or high taxes that will be not suitable for the business strategy . As the company is entering a new market, it is necessary to make sure there is an agreement on the avoidance of double taxation   ( that is  indeed  present with each given country ) , t herefore, the import taxes are  crucial  here.  It has a weight of 20%  because with high import taxes and additional charges the low-cost strategy can be disrupted.  Each factor has a different impact on the business expansion, therefore, these factors are going to have different weights in the constitution of the final results. The weight of the factor is indicated as ‘x’ of 100%. The sum is calculated as: Sum {the  rank n  x the weight of  factor k }. The relative scale of ranking is used in this paper, where 1 is the most favorable factor and 5 is the least favorable. However, in terms of the pulp and paper industry , according to  BusinesStat  (2020) , Belarus is defined as 4 th  largest importer of corrugated paper from Russia, which accounts for 3,6%  of total exports of corrugating paper from Russia  (12 200 tons). It means that there are prospective relations in terms of import-export relations between Belarus and Russia.  Licensing of import of cardboard and paper packaging  has been introduced in Belarus.  It implies that  the import into the customs territory of the Eurasian Economic Union in the Republic of Belarus of certain types of cardboard and paper packaging when placed under the customs procedure for release for domestic consumption will be carried out under one-time licenses .  That can restrain  Russian  suppliers ship larger amounts of  certain  paper-related  goods  since the final price of a product can be considerably affected.  A treaty of union  between Belarus and the Russian Federation , signed on 8 December 1998, is considered by the president of Belarus Lukashenko as a prospect of economic advantages .  It is largely dependent on Russia since the country provides discounted prices for oil and gas and lets Belarus enjoy preferential access to the Russian market.  In return Belarus is required to take concrete steps towards a political union . Overall, the political situation today seems favorable to  commit economic activities  across the border , however, is subject to cease in future due to possible disagreements between the parties .  Military operation in  Ukraine : Desiring to preserve the repressive regime and personal power at any cost, Lukashenko, the president of Belarus, not only turned the republic into an economic and political appendage of Russia, but also made it one of the key elements of the Kremlin’s foreign policy adventure, which could lead to the liquidation of the sovereign Belarusian state and risk escalating into a major war in Europe .  On February 24, Russian President Vladimir Putin announced the start of a military operation in the Donbass and the  introduction of troops into the Donetsk and Lugansk People's Republics . Belarus took part in it as a conductor of the Russian army into Ukraine. It aggravated already bad relations with Europe and other states that sanction the country.  Economic Coronavirus consequences :  due to the ongoing  pandemic, many qualitative indicators (for example, profitability of firms, debt burden, labor productivity, new jobs etc.) remain in problem ranges. This indicates that in the economy, strong prerequisites remain for a delayed and prolonged recession . For instance, in order to improve the financial situation in the country, firms have to limit output so that they could sell off existing stocks and cut down labor costs.  Negative  impact   of political crisis  on the economy :  other prerequisites to an economic recession  are  caused by the political instability in the country. Many firms and households consider the soonest future to be unfavorable for investments and large purchases. In addition, the political crisis has negative influence on the saving behavior of economic agents and thus, weakens the already fragile financial stability .   Moreover, political crisis triggered the growth of inflation in the country that evoked inconsistency in credit-deposit and foreign exchange markets causing depreciation and the exchange rate disruption.  I t is predicted that inflation in Belarus in 2022 will be 21.1%, public debt - 36.4% of GDP, and the productivity and efficiency of agriculture, industry and the service sector will also decrease . GDP fall  caused by the military  operation   in  Ukraine by Russia and Belarus : as the main ally of Russia, Belarus supports all the decisions regarding the military conflict made by the Russian go vernment. This entailed imposition  of  a number of sanctions that caused enterprises shut down. These sanctions include suspense of supply and shipment of products and raw materials into and from Belarus.  Moreover, the country incurred a decline in purchasing power  due to  the  shortage of goods and  raised prices.  Belarus' GDP in 2022 may fall by 3%. In 2023, the economy of our country will experience zero dynamics: additional sanctions and close ties  with the Russian economy will greatly affect. The EBRD assumes that in Belarus the decline in the economy will be the lowest of the three countries  (Russia, Ukraine and Belarus) . Social Change in consumer behavior :  due to the pandemic, a huge boost in the e-commerce took place and demands on goods from internet are still growing.  At pace with accelerating home delivery, the demand for cardboard is stronger than ever .  Befo re the global crisis caused by Coronavirus, Belarus  had been already  observing  the growth in demand for cardboard, packaging paper, office paper, and other paper-related products . Thus nowadays, the demand for such produce must have risen even more, making it feasible to increase the production  capabilities or amount of imported goods .  Digitalization  trend :  since the global community tends to switch to working with electronic devices, such products as newspapers and newsprint lose their usefulness. Designed for printing newspapers and supplements to them, accounting forms, bulk brochures, etc., newsprint is being replaced with digital versions that are kept in software.  Belarus, as an emerging country,  follows th is  trend, its companies are also switching to computer-based technologies , decreasing the amount of paper used.  Technological  Import -substituting technology  for the production of paper and cardboard has been developed in Belarus . It  reduces the cost of the domestic product  since the production saves expensive cellulosic raw materials (up to 10%). The use of new fillers allowed the paper to increase  whiteness and strength co mpared to imported counterparts . An innovative project for the development of fillers and technologies for their use in the pulp and paper industry  was   introduced   in  2009 .   Since then, the technology has expanded on the whole territory of Belarus and makes it harder for foreign companies to compete.  Forecasted increased paper output : the production of paper and cardboard in Belarus for 2021-2025 is planned to be increased by 2.1 times compared to 2020 which is provided for by the draft state program "Belarusian Forest"  . The expansion of production capabilities supported by the state program can have negative impact on potential importers or foreign subsidiaries.  The state invests into the technological advancement of production capabilities to achieve superiority with price and quality of pulp and paper products. In order to identify the most suitable entry strategy,  the company’s ownership, location and internalization advantages need to be analyzed.  According to the  Dunning’s  model  (see the figure below) , if there are no ownership advantages present, the company should  remain domestic ; if there are no location advantages, the company may use export as its entry strategy; if there are no internalization advantages, the firm should apply contracting /licensing ; and finally, if all the advantages are in place, the organization can implement foreign direct investment (FDI ).   OLI paradigm by J. Dunning (1980) Ownership advantages  are i nternationally transferable and enable the firm to achieve competitive advantage abroad . There are other forms of entry that are used for the expansion to the foreign market. The forms differ in terms of possession of equity. Since SBI  cannot incur any  expen ses  of establishing operations in the new country.  T he equity-based approach is not considered, thus, such strategies as Greenfield, Brownfield, Acquisition, and Joint Venture  fall off. Non-equity approach includes Direct Export, Licensing and Franchising , that will be compared.  Exporting  is the marketing and direct sale of domestically produced goods in another country. Exporting is a traditional and well-established method of reaching foreign markets. Licensing  essentially permits a company in the target country to use the property of the licensor. Such property is usually intangible, such as trademarks, patents, and production techniques . In addition to the normal license process a company provides assistance with the design, equipment, organization and marketing of a contractual partner in the target market . They are presented below : Find a customer  through its existent marketing channels.  There are two ways it can be done: the customer on its own finds  Soyuzblankizdat  and reaches it via email or phone  (the company SBI is represented on different pulp and paper fairs where it can get contacted) ; or SBI, while taking part in an international fair of pulp and paper producers, contacts different companies and offers its goods.  Recently, INCI group – Azerbaijani company that  distributes different types of products has shown its interest in the Russian paper producers. SBI can delicately reach the representatives of this distributor, prove its competence and advantages over others and become the first importer.  Inci  group is the distributor of paper across a number of Azerbaijani firms, that include  the competitors of SBI on this market – ASSIS and CASSA.  There are other companies present on the Azerbaijani market that could also possibly form cooperation with SBI. Those include  Ofisait ,  BMSGL ,  Oyal  and  Texnoev .  Determine a  single product  with which the company can start expansion. Since SBI is not familiar with the foreign market, it needs to supply only one category of its good and understand whether the market will accept it.  The good will be determined by the customer, however, it can be observed that there are product groups that are  presented on the market  by a few companies only and can be demanded. Request and receive certifications on the product  for the export.  Certificate of origin   of goods form ST-1   is  a document certifying that a certain batch of products originates from a member country of the Union of Independent States. The presence of this document affects the application of measures of tariff and non-tariff customs regulation.   In relation to goods, the free trade regime can be applied (restored) in the customs territories of the states-participants of the Agreement   (Azerbaijan is a member of this agreement).  Sign a  contract  with all the specifications , terms   and details  of the export (delivery) process.  Since the companies are situated in different locations (countries), the primary negotiation will take place online or via phone (if the representatives of the client are not present in Saint-Petersburg), then the original s of  contracts are signed  in two exemplars.  Receive an  advance payment  ( 30 % of the total price).   This is not certain, but preferred by SBI. Usually partners agree on these terms.  Request  services of a  customs representative  (broker)  for customs clearance  (declaration on goods, examination and monitoring of goods).   Ship the goods  to the destination agreed in the contact with a proper control over the transportation.  SBI has agreements with transportation services in the Russian market, however, in order to perform the transportation to Azerbaijan, it has decided that the client will be responsible for the payment for transpo rtation.  In case the supplier refuses to use its own transportation, SBI can change its policy and become responsible for goods delivery through outsourced transportation.  In addition, the contracts as well as the certificates of the origin are sent with the freight. It is done in order to have SBI’s expenses minimized (the delivery services will not be engaged in this process).   Receive the rest of the  payment .  The expansion to the Azerbaijani market will be conducted through one category of goods :  colored paper (yellow pastel) in A4 format, 80 grams (density), 500 sheets in a box in the volume of 20 tons (1 fully loaded truck) . The reason why only one product is chosen is that SBI will need to evaluate whether its performance on that market will be profitable. Moreover, for each product the strategy needs  to  be unique  and financial indicators are to be corrected.  Exactly this product is chosen since it is not widely presented on the market.  At the  Ofisait  website, one of the biggest platforms selling stationery (both wholesale and retail),  there are no such products presented, making it possible for SBI to take the niche  and become an exclusive supplier of yellow paper.  As it is noted in the comparison of assortment of SBI’s competitors Assis,  Cassa  and SL, they do not offer colored paper too.  3.2.2  Business  m odel  c anvas  a daptation The business model of  Soyuzblankizdat  will incur changes due to the Azerbaijani market entry. The changes are described as follows: Customer segments The  customer segment itself is not going to be altered a lot: it will be a wholesale buyer company that is interested in both customized and basic goods. However, no w  it is going to be a n Azerbaijani  company that may speak different language.  Value   proposition   Soyuzblankizdat  is going to start its expansion with delivering large volumes of yellow paper (20 tons per shipment) .  It means that the low price and economies of scale will still take place, however, the wide assortment is moved to the background. In order to successfully perform on the Azerbaijani market, the company SBI will offer a wide range of products that will be introduced gradually. That is why, for the beginning of its expansion, SBI will offer 1 product category, that is colored (yellow pastel) paper with the following characteristics: A4 format, 80 gram density, 500 sheets in a box.  Channels The company SBI uses direct marketing as a channel and it has decided not to use any other marketing strategies since it could end up adding a large amount of costs that would have a negative effect on the final price of the product. Thus, the company, through a new affiliated entity  (client manager whose focus is on the Azerbaijani market), is going to market customers at international  pulp and paper  forums and fairs which it has not attended before  (usually take place in the foreign country or in Moscow, Russia) .  Customer relations The relations with customers are going to  remain the same –  Negotiation ,  Ordering ,  Price reconciliation ,  Contracting ,  Order tracking ,  Defects & returns . However, in order to find and maintain relations with the Azerbaijani customers,  the company needs a client  (sales)  manager that is well familiar with the market and  might  have foreign language efficiency  (in case the Azerbaijani customer is non-Russian speaker) .  Revenue streams In general, r evenue streams  are going to be the same – the company will receive payments in RUB through the bank transfer.  After the contract is signed, SBI receives 30% of the total amount stated, then the goods are shipped and upon the arrival to the final destination SBI receives the rest of the payment (70%).  Key resources The most significant change will take place at the human resources as the company will need to hire a manager who will be strictly working with the Azerbaijani market.  He or she will be responsible for the communication, registration and certification, shipment and control of this direction. Manufacturing facilities will remain the same as there is space left for the capacity  growth.  The required competences include: experience in sales of paper product (local or international), knowledge of the industry,  mathematical mindset. The employee will be situated at the main (and only) office in Saint Petersburg,  Bumazhnaya  street 17, and will be provided with all needed space and equipment.  Key activities The activities performed by the company will be expanded  to the Azerbaijani market; this includes:  increase of production volume, and extension of customer service and performance control through the introduction of a new client (sales) manager.  The control over the Azerbaijani market is not needed since a new partner (customer) will be responsible for the further retail of the produce.  Key partners The company SBI will continue ordering raw materials from the its suppliers; the typographies and production facilities will also stay un changed. As for transportation services, SBI has decided that the purchasing company will  be responsible for transportation and  bear shipment costs.  Thus, it will not use the services of transport companies.  In a unique case when SBI is responsible for the delivery of goods, its partner base will be enlarged with the transportation companies. In addition, broker services are going to be used on an annual basis and become another key partner. Thus,  the company  has decided to increase the scope of its operations and choose  one  of the  six  CIS countries where its products could be demanded.  A number of factors have been analysed in order to narrow the selection from six to two markets – Belarus and Azerbaijan, that would require deeper examination. Further ,  having completed  a  profound  scanning of both external and competitive environments, the market of the republic of Azerbaijan proved to be more favorable  for the company’s entry . Its political situation is specified to be more reliable and secure in comparison to the  one of  Belarusian.  The economic factors indicate that there are many prospects for development in this market, while Belarussian has more constraints in terms of doing business there. Social and technological aspects tend to give more opportunities for SBI on the market of Azerbaijan since the republic is less developed and  entails more potential in business conduction . All these external factors  indicate a gradual and predictable  evolution   of the market and   contribute  to a favourable transition of the company’s assets to the market.  Azerbaijani  competitive environment  has its disadvantages , such as required capital investments  and  low product differentiation , but in general it has  conducive  prospects  for entry, such as  lack of pulp and paper mills,  non-saturated market, and  low switching costs for buyers.  Comparing its competitive environment to the one of Belarus, I could conclude that the Belarusian pulp and paper industry is highly developed inasmuch as  there are many large  companies  that  possess special production technologies  and  offer paper goods of a significantly lower price .  Furthermore, t here are a lot of entry requirements to the Belarusian pulp and paper industry, which cannot be achieved by SBI.  When entering the Azerbaijani industry, SBI will be able to keep its competitive advantage of low price segment obtained through economies of scale  since SBI’s price will be lower than the market average, the assortment proves to be larger than the one of its competitors and SBI has a direct access to raw materials .  The entry  will be done through the use of export strategy for which only a few initial investments are required. In general, the costs will grow insignificantly  and  will be minimized, which will allow the company target Azerbaijani customers and carry on low cost strategy  while offering the clients high quality and cheap produce.  Describing and evaluating the entry strategy itself, I can state that the company’s activities are not going to alter significantly. The main change will take  place  with the human resources since SBI will need a specialist who will be responsible for international (Azerbaijani)  relations. He or she will be also responsible for the organization and obtainment of all needed for export documents (e.g. certificates of product origin), while customs clearance, declaration and related processes are going to be accomplished by broker agency. Moreover, the capacity of the production facility will be increased due to the enlarged volume of output. To sum up, c onsidering the scope of company’s activities, these are  minor  things that have to be introduced, howev er, should be done  thoroughly and well-planned .  I believe this paper achieved its goal of finding the optimal market for the entry by SBI. Moreover, there was given a detailed description of the company and its competitive advantage. The managerial problem was defined as well as the methodology. There was provided the extensive selection of entry markets and 2 best ones were chosen. The analyses  and frameworks used in the paper  were PEST, Porter’s 5 forces, SWOT , OLI ,  Business model canvas  and scenario analysis . Finally, the entry strategy was defined and evaluated.  To sum up, the optimal entry strategy on the foreign market  for LLC  Soyuzblankizdat   has been  defined  through a number of analyses, deep research in the industry and application of managerial knowledge and skills. Цели:
- Улучшение процесса международных поставок бумаги.
- Оптимизация цен на услуги.

Задачи:
- Анализ рынка бумаги в различных странах мира.
- Выбор наиболее подходящих стран для входа на рынок.
- Оценка конкурентоспособности компаний на выбранном рынке.
- Определение финансовых возможностей для входа на новый рынок.
- Разработка стратегии для успешного выхода на новый рынок.
- Поиск партнеров и клиентов для расширения бизнеса.
- Оптимизация процессов международных поставок бумаги с целью снижения затрат и улучшения качества обслуживания клиентов.
- Разработка системы оценки эффективности работы компании на международных рынках.
